entry.title: Use of Pegging for Demand-to-Procurement Traceability in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
I am currently facing a challenge in the area of MRP and procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition.
In my scenario, I have multiple demands (e.g. reservations A, B, and C). Due to a supplier’s minimum order quantity (e.g. X pieces), I procure the required quantity to stock via a purchase order.
My key question is:
How can I transparently identify which purchase order (e.g. PO 123 or PO 127 or PO 129) is actually covering which specific demand (e.g. material Y for reservation A)?
From my perspective, the standard stock/requirements list (MD04) is not sufficient for this purpose.
I understand that pegging could potentially provide better transparency, as it logically links receipts and requirements and represents the material flow structure.
However, I have several questions in this context:
1. How is advanced planning (PP/DS) activated and licensed in the Public Cloud environment (screenshot below)?
2. Are there recommended best practices or alternative approaches to achieve end-to-end transparency between demand and procurement elements?
I am aware that dynamic pegging can be recalculated during planning runs and therefore may not provide stable traceability, whereas fixed pegging would better support this requirement.
I would highly appreciate any insights, recommendations, or practical experiences regarding this topic.
Thank you in advance.
Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: The Flyweel is turning: Partners leverage SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP BTP during Hack2Build
entry.content:
In the fast-paced digital landscape of 2025, SAP unveiled its plans for SAP Business Data Cloud as well as SAP HANA Cloud at SAP TechEd 2025, positioning itself as a leading force in data-driven innovation. Building on these announcements, SAP’s latest Hack2Build event explored the full potential of building applications on SAP Business Data Cloud.

The Hack2Build on Building Applications on SAP Business Data Cloud provided an immersive environment for partners to leverage the advanced capabilities of SAP Business Data Cloud as well as HANA Cloud and BTP, thus tapping even into the realm of agentic AI.  Equipped with hands-on coaching from SAP experts, participating partners became frontrunners in leveraging cutting-edge technologies, empowering them to transform insights into actionable solutions.

During the Hack2Build initiative, partners engaged in a seven-day hack phase, constructing functional applications that harnessed the power of real-time data analytics, advanced AI-driven business intelligence and actionable insights. The event emphasized seamless integration with SAP and non-SAP systems, and the capability to translate raw data into analytical insights and from there into strategic actions.

The culmination of the event featured a Live Demo Jam, where partners presented their innovative prototypes to a panel of SAP judges. This collaborative event showcased the ingenuity and potential of SAP's latest technology advancements, paving the way for future applications that bridge the gap between data insights and actionable business strategies.

It was again amazing to see how partners turned their innovative solution visions into running prototypes within seven days and delivering even full breakthroughs from sourcing the data through analytical modeling into leveraging the insights in Joule agents. The flywheel is truly turning now!

Participating Partners:

Participating Partners in the Hack2Build Building Applications on BDC, Apr 22-29 2026

🏆Winners

With that momentum in motion, the judges selected three solutions that stood out across technical completeness, workflow clarity, and customer relevance.

🥇NTT Data Business Solutions 

Manufacturers often face unexpected disruptions that reduce operational efficiency and can delay customer deliveries. The Intelligent Manufacturing solution addresses this by using SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform to combine data from SAP ERP, SAP Data Products, custom Data Products, machine telemetry, and energy consumption. These data sources are used to train AI models in SAP Databricks, enabling early detection of production orders at risk due to supplier delays, plant failures, equipment issues, lack of resources, or other factors. The solution provides actionable insights through SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Build, while SAP Build Process Automation triggers real-time notifications for risk mitigation.

 

🥈cbs Consulting

cbs solution AI-Driven Dynamic Safety Stock Optimization aims at transforming supply chain resilience using SAP BDC, BTP, and AI. By integrating SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP data with external market drivers (Oil prices, Forex Rates, & Trade Routes) via SAP Datasphere, the data is harmonized, while predictive models from SAP Databricks assess demand variability, supply risk, and external factors to generate optimal stock recommendations. SAP Analytics Cloud provides simulations, insights, and planning capabilities, while SAP Build Apps triggers the multi-level approval workflow and operationalise decisions back into core systems (SAP S/4HANA). The result? A closed-loop, intelligent process that reduces excess inventory and boosts market responsiveness.

 

🥉SDG Group

In oil and gas, an unplanned equipment failure costs $45,000–$60,000 per hour. The manual response cycle stretches that loss across 48 hours on average. Rig-Guard consolidates historical NPT records and live sensor readings (temperature, pressure, vibration) into a single source of truth on SAP BDC, then runs a predictive ML model on SAP Databricks to catch failures before they happen. When a prediction fires, an agentic workflow on SAP Build Process Automation takes it from insight to action: Autonomous above 95% confidence, Human in the Loop below - failure caught, revenue protected. The rig watches itself. The rig never stops.

 

⭐ Finalists

All other finalists proved themselves to be winners in their own right through the enthusiasm and creativity they demonstrated, underscoring the vitality of SAP's partner ecosystem. Across industries such as finance, supply chain, retail, and more, these accomplished partners delivered BDC-based solutions with clear business relevance and strong potential for rapid customer adoption. Their impressive prototypes showcased how Applications, Data & AI come to life and truly turn the flywheel.

 

Partner Company

Solution Title

All for One

AI Planning Insight Agent for SAP Analytics Cloud & Datasphere

cbs

AI-Driven Dynamic Safety Stock Optimization

Deloitte

CFO Impact Analyzer

DXC

SAP Customer Delivery Preference Identification

EY

MarketIQ

IBM

Revenue Timing & Value Conflicts with Tariff Resolution

Infosys

Infosys Size Curve Prediction Solution for Retail

KPMG

AI Copilot for Real-Time Supplier Risk & Sourcing Decisions

NTT DATA Business Solutions

Manufacturing Intelligence

PWC

24/7 Reconciliation & Monitoring

 

What’s Next

All participating partners now move into a six‑week Build Phase, where they will refine and extend their prototypes with support from SAP product management, Partner Ecosystem Success as well as many other experts in BDC, BTP and AI. This phase will focus on preparing solutions for customer validation and potential go‑to‑market discussions.

Acknowledgments

Many thanks to all partner teams, Judges, Databricks team and SAP contributors across Business Data Cloud Product Management, Customer Success & Delivery, Value Prototyping, BTP, BDC and AI Solution Architects, Partner Excellence Center Leads and Partner Ecosystem Success for making this edition of Hack2Build a milestone in spinning the flywheel of Applications, Data & AI!

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026 - Join me in our SAP BDC Sessions!
entry.content:
A couple of words about me: 

Hi, I'm Prakash! I've spent the last 30+ years in the enterprise software world, driven by one passion: turning messy, complex data into something teams can actually trust and act on. I founded Paxata to give customers a simpler, self-service way to prepare data at scale, and before that started Velosel focused on master data management. Most recently, I led DocuSign's Emerging Products & Technology group as Global VP and GM, where my team built the data fabric, AI, search, and analytics platform powering their applications. Today, I have the incredible privilege of leading SAP Business Data Cloud and I can tell you, this is the most exciting chapter yet. We are on a quest to make SAP BDC the enterprise data platform of choice for the agentic era, and we are just getting started!!

I am thrilled to share with you my sessions at SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026. I would love to see you there!

Accelerate insights across your organization with SAP Business Data Cloud | BDC2061
This one is close to my heart. We'll dive into how SAP Business Data Cloud brings SAP data and intelligence together: curated metrics, built-in AI models, and planning capabilities that you can actually put to work today. The focus: accelerating real, high-impact business scenarios like improving working capital or combining workforce insights across SAP and non-SAP sources. This is the power of contextual, business-ready data in action.

Getting to know data products in SAP Business Data Cloud | BDC2747
The fastest path to value? Start with data products that already "know" your business context. In this session, we'll cover what SAP data products are, how they fit into your landscape, and why they matter for both business and IT leaders. If you want to see how SAP Business Data Cloud delivers trusted, AI-ready data at scale—this is the session for you!

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic: 
SAP Business Data Cloud

Related community topic pages: 
SAP Business Data Cloud

Looking forward to seeing you: 
If you're at Sapphire Orlando, I would love for you to join me and the team. Across these sessions, we'll get deep into how SAP Business Data Cloud helps you bring together SAP and non-SAP data, work with curated business-ready data products, and put AI and planning capabilities to use for real scenarios - from faster insights to better decisions. Deep gratitude to our incredible customers, partners, and SAP colleagues who make this journey possible. See you in Orlando!!!

entry.title: What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud Modeling Integration & Calculations QRC2 2026 Edition
entry.content:
Purpose

Hello SAP Community Colleagues,

With SAP Analytics Cloud QRC2 2026 edition, you can unlock powerful insights into your data exports with the introduction of the Data Export API and Delta Calculation jobs in Job Monitor.

Read on to learn more about this new monitoring capability, along with our additional SAP Business Data Cloud modeling highlights!!

Overview

Here is a short overview of each feature:

Data Integration Topics

  1. Introduction of Data Export API & Delta Calculations into Job Monitor - The monitoring of Data Extraction & Delta Calculation jobs is now possible within Job Monitor of SAP Analytics Cloud.
  2. Data Import API – Import of Fact Data into Private Versions in Seamless Planning Models - Data Import API has been extended to allow for the import of fact data to existing private versions on a Seamless Planning model.
  3. Data Import API – Import of Master Data into Seamless Planning Public Dimensions - Data Import API has been extended to allow for the import of master data into Public Dimensions that resides in a SAP Datasphere space.

Calculations Topics

  1. Enhancements to Average and Count Exception Aggregation Formulas - The Average and Count exception aggregation formulas now appear as Average incl. 0, NULL and Count incl. 0, NULL in model calculations.
  2. Introduction of Member Selector Dialog for Unit artifacts in Modeling - A new Member Selector dialog replaces the existing value help on base measures, calculated measures and dimension properties of type “unit”.
  3. Enhancements to Data Entry on Inverse Formulas - Data entry is now possible on inverse formulas where the non-target operand contains also contains a formula.

Let’s look at the Data Integration Topics in further detail:

  1. Introduction of Data Export API, Data Import API & Delta Calculations into Job Monitor

The Data Export and Import APIs within SAP Analytics Cloud  facilities the bi-directional workflow of data to and from other SAP Products and 3rd party tools.  Until now, visibility of the status of API running jobs was available within the API Client only, and not from within SAP Analytics Cloud.

With the introduction of the Data Export API - Data Extraction & Delta Calculation jobs into Job Monitor, customers are provided with enhanced observability and self-monitoring capabilities of their API jobs.

Learn more about this feature with our SAP Business Data Cloud video:

  • Data Export API in Job Monitor

Data Extraction and API Subscription jobs that are part of the Data Export API can now be monitored within Job Monitor of SAP Analytics Cloud.   

Data Extraction API Jobs in Job Monitor

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Authorized users can now gain insights into the status of their jobs within a centralized location of SAP Analytics Cloud.  Useful information such as API running times, job status, number of extracted records & job settings are readily available within this easy to navigate interface. 

Data extraction jobs include Master Data, Fact Data, Public Dimension Data, Audit, Currency & Unit.

A new Source Object column under the Data Export API  tab lists the object from where the data is being extracted from.  Objects are hyperlinked to provide navigation back to the source. Use the filter and search options in Job Monitor to locate a specific job.

Select an individual job to gain further insights, such as the number of records that have been exported, the job duration and API configuration details used as part of the OData extraction criteria.

Users with the Job Monitor Administration role can monitor all jobs running within Job Monitor, including Data Export & Delta Calculation jobs.

Users with read access to an artifact, including a Standard or Seamless Planning model, public dimension, currency rate table, standard unit table or unit description table can view all extraction jobs for that specific object inside Job Monitor. 

The retention period for Data Extraction jobs is 7 days.

  • Delta Calculations in Job Monitor

As part of Data Export API, model fact data delta changes that are pulled to a target system, such as SAP S/4 HANA, are performed using Delta Calculations jobs. 

Delta Calculation jobs are created when delta subscriptions are activated on a model.  These jobs are automatically generated to capture fact data delta changes.  During the busy planning cycle, they can be numerous and long running.  Until now, there was no visibility of the status of these jobs.

With the introduction of Delta Calculation jobs in Job Monitor of SAP Analytics Cloud, customers benefit from better job visibility & transparency of these types of jobs.

Delta Calculation Jobs in Job Monitor

Authorized users can select Delta Calculations within the Data Export API tab to observe the status of these jobs. 

View Delta Calculation Job Details

Each job includes useful information such as status, duration, owner, provider name & ID information. Jobs can be filtered by status, or on any of the alternative column headings, to help narrow the volume of logs being displayed. Individual jobs can be selected to display more detailed information on running times and status. The lazy-loading capabilities of Job Monitor  handles the display of multiple jobs. 

The retention period for Delta Calculation jobs is 24 hours.

Check out the SAP Analytics Cloud REST API Guide for further information on how to leverage the power of Data Export and Data Import API to meet your data integration needs for SAP Analytics Cloud within Business Data Cloud.

2. Data Import API – Import into Models Containing Single Column Dimensions

What are single column dimensions? -  In a model, single-column dimensions denote dimensions without an associated storage table or master data.

What’s new with Single Column Dimensions? - The Data Import API has been extended to allow for the retrieval of metadata and the running of a fact data import job into an SAC standard model containing single column dimensions.

Existing endpoints can be used for these scenarios:

  • {BaseURL}/providers/{NamespaceID}/{ProviderID}/$metadata 
  • {BaseURL}/providers/{NamespaceID}/{ProviderID}/FactData

This import type is supported using a 2-legged, machine-to-machine connection, & 3-legged, user-authenticated connection.  The Data Import API now supports imports into SAC Standard Models and Seamless Planning models with dimension tables that include master data, as well as single-column dimensions without master data, delivering further feature parity with this feature.

3. Data Import API – Import of Fact Data into Private Versions in Seamless Planning Models

The Data Import API has been extended to allow for the import of fact data coming from external sources to an existing private version on a Seamless Planning model.  This aligns with how the Data Import API imports fact data to private versions on SAC standard models. The existing endpoint can be used for this import scenario:

  • {BaseURL}/providers/{NamespaceID}/{ProviderID}/FactData

It is supported using a 3-legged connection only, as private versions as specific to individual users.  Imports using JSON and CSV formats are also supported.  Checks are in place to ensure users have access to a Datasphere space and that the space is not locked.

4. Data Import API – Import of Master Data into Seamless Planning Public Dimensions

The Data Import API has been extended to allow for the import of master data into Public Dimensions that resides in a Datasphere space.  This aligns with how the Data Import API imports master data into public dimensions residing in SAP Analytics Cloud standalone tenants. The existing endpoint can be used for this import scenario:

  • {BaseURL}/providers/{NamespaceID}/{ProviderID}/MasterData

It is supported using 2-legged (machine to machine) and 3-legged (user authenticated) connections. Imports using JSON and CSV formats are also supported. Checks are in place to ensure users have access to a Datasphere space and that the space is not locked.

Let’s look at the Model Calculation Topics in further detail:

  1. Enhancements to Average and Count Exception Aggregation

The Average and Count exception aggregation formulas now appear as Average incl. 0, NULL and Count incl. 0, NULL within the SAP Analytics Cloud model.  Formulas that use an exception aggregation of AVERAGE or COUNT on base measures and base accounts have been enhanced to display AVERAGE incl. 0, NULL and COUNT incl. 0, NULL, respectively. 

Updates to Count & Average Exception Aggregations

This provides clearer visibility of what data is included within formulas using these exception aggregation functions.  It is recommended to avoid selecting AVERAGE incl. 0, NULL as the default average exception aggregation value, as results can be impacted by other measures, which can sometimes be misleading.   Further details are available in SAP Note 3695342 Understanding Exception Aggregation Average incl. 0, NULL in SAP Analytics Cloud

In addition, it is now possible to create an AVERAGENULL, AVERAGENULLZERO, COUNTNULL & COUNTNULLZERO on base accounts without the need for a  formula.  The below restriction has been removed.

The formula requirement on exception aggregations has been removed for account members

See also the following blog Understanding Average Exception Aggregation in SAP Analytics Cloud by Alecsandra Dimofte explaining average exception aggregation behaviours.

2. Introduction of Member Selector Dialog for Calculations and Unit in Modeling

The member selector dialog is now available for base measures, calculated measures, conversion measures, account members and public dimensions of type unit.

Member Select Dialog is now available for all unit types in the SAC model

With this enhancement, modelers can now reference unit types listed within the standard unit table without leaving their model.

3.  Data entry on inverse formulas improvements

The Inverse formula has been enhanced to support data entry on calculations where the non-target operand contains a formula.  For example, in the below calculation, input enablement is allowed on base measures or accounts.

A = B * C | Inverse( B := A / C )

If the calculation is changed to include a calculated measure or account, for example A = B * D | Inverse( B := A / D ), where D consists of a formula, input enablement is now also permitted.  Note that there are some limitations to this.  For example, if ‘D’ consists of an exception aggregation, a nested formula, a compounded formula, data entry will not be available.

Conclusion

This upcoming QRC2 2026 edition of SAP Analytics Cloud contains an action packed list of features for Modeling Integration & Calculations !!

  1. Introduction of Data Export API & Delta Calculations into Job Monitor
  2. Data Import API – Import of Fact Data into Private Versions in Seamless Planning Models
  3. Data Import API – Import of Master Data into Seamless Planning Public Dimensions
  4. Enhancements to Average and Count Exception Aggregation
  5. Introduction of Member Selector Dialog for Calculations and Unit in Modeling
  6. Enhancements to Data Entry on Inverse Formulas

A comprehensive list of SAC features for each QRC release can be found in our What's New - 2026 Releases Guide.  

If you have any questions, feel free to comment below or post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum.   

See this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

For further information, visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. 

Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Until next time, thanks a lot for reading!

I’m encountering a 500 Internal Server Error in SAP Business ByDesign while working on a tax declaration process.

Error details:

  • Message: 500 Internal Server Error

  • Tag: {0058b1d6}

  • Timestamp: May 6, 2026 – 16:00:45

  • Context: Occurs during execution of a tax declaration transaction (generation/validation step)

What I’ve checked so far:

  • Fiscal period appears to be open

  • No obvious inconsistencies in master data (business partners, tax codes, etc.)

  • Issue persists after retrying the transaction

Questions:

  1. Has anyone experienced a similar error during tax declaration processes in ByDesign?

  2. Could this be related to inconsistent tax data, document issues, or backend processing errors?

  3. Is there any way to trace or diagnose this from the ByD frontend (e.g., business logs), or is SAP support required to analyze the tag?

Any guidance on how to troubleshoot or isolate the root cause would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Can anyone provide insight into how to get this working?

SELECT
{prod.code},

entry.title: Per-row Gross Total (LC) ≠ Total + Tax Amount due to Gross Price rounding at 2 decimals - SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm seeing a small but persistent rounding discrepancy in the per-row Gross Total (LC) column of A/R Invoices, and I'd like to know what alternatives the community has used without changing the Price decimal places at the system level.

System Information

  • SAP Business One version: 10.0
  • Patch Level: FP 2502
  • Localization: South Africa
  • VAT rate involved: 15%
  • Decimal Places - Prices: 2, Amounts: 2, Rates: 4
  • Rounding Method: By Currency
  • Pricing Mode: Net

The Issue

For document rows where tax applies, SAP calculates the per-row Gross Total as:

 
Gross Total = ROUND(Unit Price × (1 + Tax%), 2) × Quantity

This produces a different result than Total (LC) + Tax Amount (LC) because the Gross Price is rounded to 2 decimals before being multiplied by quantity. The discrepancy scales linearly with quantity - so on high-quantity rows, the deviation grows proportionally.

Example 

Field Value
Unit Price (Net)R 910.37
Quantity80
Tax CodeO1 (15%)
Total (LC)R 72,829.60
Tax Amount (LC)R 10,924.44
Total + Tax (calculated)R 83,754.04
Gross Price after Disc. (SAP)R 1,046.93 (910.37 × 1.15 = 1,046.9255 rounded to 2dp)
Gross Total (LC) shown by SAPR 83,754.40 (1,046.93 × 80)
DiscrepancyR 0.36

The per-unit rounding loss of 0.0045 multiplies out to 0.36 at quantity 80. On rows with quantities of 100+, this can exceed R 0.50 per line. Across a multi-line invoice, cumulative DIFF often lands between R 0.10 and R 0.80 per document.

The document footer Total reconciles correctly via the Net mechanism (Sum of Total + Sum of Tax), so the GL, AR, and VAT reporting are unaffected. Only the per-row Gross Total column carries the rounding artifact.

What I've Already Confirmed

  • This is documented behavior per the official "How to Work with Gross Prices" guide ("small differences may occur between the sum of the amounts appearing in the Gross Total field in the document rows and the amount appearing in the Total field in the document footer")
  • The linked price lists are confirmed in Net mode
  • The "Round Tax Amount in Rows" checkbox is not available in SA localization
  • Increasing Price decimal places to 4 would resolve it mathematically, but I'm avoiding that route due to its irreversibility and impact on existing PLDs / Crystal Reports / add-on integrations

My Question to the Community

Has anyone resolved this without increasing Price decimal places? Specifically:

  1. Is there any hidden / SAP-Support-activatable flag that changes the per-row Gross Total formula to LineTotal + VatSum instead of GrossPrice × Quantity?
  2. For the SA localization specifically, is there any locale-level setting that affects this calculation order?
  3. Has anyone successfully argued for a "by-design" exception with their auditor when the per-row Gross Total drifts by a few cents (or in high-quantity cases, multiple rands) from Total + Tax?

Any insight from fellow SA-localization users or anyone who has dealt with this in KR/BR/IN would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,
Best Regards,
Sunil Patil
Certified SAP Business One Consultant

entry.title: DEMO: Smarter Master Data Creation with Automation
entry.content:
Managing master data accurately and efficiently is a challenge every organization faces. Manual data entry is time-consuming, error-prone, and often leads to duplicates that can disrupt business processes. But what if you could eliminate that manual effort entirely — right from the very first step?

In this video, we explore how SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) integrates seamlessly with external data providers like CDQ to revolutionize the way business partners are created and enriched. Using a lookup-and-create model, users can search for a business partner, pull verified reference data from multiple trusted sources, and instantly generate a golden record — all without manual data entry.

You’ll see how confidence scores, multi-source enrichment, and side-by-side data comparison work together to ensure only the highest quality data enters your system. The result? A clean, verified business partner record that’s governance-ready from day one.

Whether you’re looking to improve data quality, reduce duplication, or accelerate your master data workflows, this demonstration offers a compelling look at what modern, automated data governance can achieve.

entry.title: UMB operation and CKMLCP: PUP goes back to old price
entry.content:
Hi experts,

In S4HANA Public Cloud client, with actual costing activated, for a 3S Material:

If in a month X I have an initial stock of 1,000 units at €1/unit, I revalue it with MR21 to €1.2/unit (entry: €200 (balance account/BSX) to (price difference account/UMB) €200), and there are no further goods movements, when I run the ML closing it will post that price difference back to inventory (€200 (acct. price difference/PRY) to (acct. 300*/BSX) €200). In other words, the inventory value at the end of the month (PUP, AT actual valuation) will go back to the initial price of €1 instead of €1.2, which is the correct one.

Does that make sense?

In the following month (X+1), with the second posting made by ML, it will reverse that last entry (BSX to PRY), so it will go back to €1.2 (correct), but the price difference will be carried forward to this month X+1, which means that when ML is run again for month X+1, if there are no movements neither, the PUP will drop again to €1 in the month-end valuation!

This is making me question whether the postings made when you perform an MR21 or release a standard cost (CK40N) actually make sense.

PD: All this despite knowing that standard cost will always be 1,2€ from MR21 execution on.

Is this the standard way of posting MR21 and CK40N release? What can I do so that PUP becomes the correct price 1,2€?

entry.title: Re: From RAP to Mobile: Reusing OData V4 Services with SAP Mobile Services and MDK
entry.content:
Hi @Burcu_Karlidag,


Thanks for such a straightforward use case!
 
In this scenario, we’re essentially using RAP as the OData/service provider. Thinking it through: we don't necessarily need MDK, right? A Fiori Elements app should work just fine on mobile devices. 
 
Plus, combining RAP Business Objects and services with Mobile Services and Build Work Zone (via the Mobile Start app) seems like a solid plan. We’d be skipping MDK—and its low-code/no-code perks—but since we can build Fiori Elements apps directly with RAP services, MDK feels optional here. 
 
One quick thought: if we’re building for mobile, we definitely need draft capabilities. Most mobile apps have that feature nowadays, so it’ll be a must-have for our users! 

Thanks,
Tuncay

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: Unlocking Next-Level Innovation to AI with SAP’s Platform
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

Mark your calendars – SAP Sapphire 2026 is here, and it's set to redefine what's possible. This year, the conversation isn’t just about digital transformation. The focus is on mastering your platform journey to drive continuous innovation, achieve measurable business outcomes, and on how organizations should leverage artificial intelligence. It’s going to be a fantastic opportunity to learn, share together, and of course, I’m especially looking forward to celebrating with everyone at the afterparty. I just found out there’s hazelnut chocolate fruit waffles at Magic Kingdom, so I’m extra excited!

From AI-powered development and intelligent automation to seamless integration and strategic platform management, SAP’s platform stands as the essential foundation. Its unique differentiation lies in deeply embedding AI across your operations while preserving the invaluable data and business application context, thereby turning challenges into tangible opportunities. Sapphire 2026 will be the pivotal event to launch SAP’s strategic vision, highlighting how AI innovations are integral to the enterprises of tomorrow.

 

The Mandate: Connecting Everything, Across the Entire Landscape

In an era when disconnected landscapes continue to hinder progress, SAP’s customers have consistently shown that the platform's true power lies in end-to-end connectivity. Whether it's your CHRO leveraging SAP SuccessFactors employee data with procurement data for newly onboarding employees, or your CFO optimizing financials with SAP S/4HANA together with external third-party benchmark data, or your CPO driving procurement excellence by keeping warehouse data up to date from supply chain partners, the challenge remains: how do you connect these vital functions, including between the AI agents, to move beyond implementation to true business transformation?

This year, you’ll see how SAP’s platform serves as the critical foundation for SAP’s business applications, making this connectivity a reality. It's designed to streamline operations, foster innovation, and enable real-time analysis of insights across your entire business landscape.

 

Spotlight on Transformation: Breakthrough Use Cases for a Connected Future

This Sapphire, we're particularly excited to unveil new use cases that exemplify the platform’s power to address today's most pressing business challenges.

 

1. Integrate HR data and processes with government and third‑party systems

  • The Challenge: Navigating complex and evolving HR regulations, increasing demands for efficiency in HR service delivery, and managing diverse workforces effectively.
  • How the platform helps: By integrating HR data in SAP SuccessFactors (or elsewhere) and processes with government and third-party systems through SAP Integration Suite, organizations can ensure compliance with global and local regulations, automate processes, and streamline interactions with third-parties. This connected process frees the HR department from administrative burdens, creating streamlined employee experiences and compliant data.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Proving the business value of standardizing on SAP Integration Suite | BTP2350: Hear how teams quantify business value after standardizing on SAP Integration Suite: before-and-after metrics, faster time to integrate, fewer incidents, lower operating effort, and readiness for a mixed SAP and third-party software landscape. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • PPG Industries: Building an AI-ready, clean core integration foundation | BTP2638: See how APIs, events, and cloud integration reduce complexity, improve visibility, and establish an AI-ready foundation for future innovation across SAP and third-party systems. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Building a foundation for AI with an integrated core HR system | HCM1742: Learn how a connected approach to core HR, payroll, workforce management, and HR service delivery can help you streamline your people and business processes while enabling AI readiness. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

2. Drive profitable growth with secure, personalized digital experiences

  • The Challenge: Organizations struggle with complex processes and fragmented access to customer data, facing increasing competition and rising customer expectations. Data silos often lead to slow and reactive decision-making, impacting customer satisfaction and growth.
  • How the platform helps: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management (SAP CIAM) protects sensitive customer data and meets global compliance requirements. By consolidating and integrating customer data across various systems with SAP’s platform, it enables the creation of secure, personalized digital experiences that overcome data silos. This supports dynamic and tailored customer interactions, enhancing customer acquisition and retention.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Standardize, secure, and scale with core platform capabilities | BTP1315: Learn how application lifecycle management, security, and interoperability unlock SAP’s Platform to drive agility and growth. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Invisible security: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management | BTP1316: Discover how SAP Customer Identity and Access Management accelerates your business by harnessing AI-assisted risk analysis to stop threats in real time, protecting customers behind the scenes without disrupting experiences. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

3. Leverage AI Capabilities in ABAP Cloud Development

  • The Challenge: Developers struggle to balance rapid delivery with maintaining code standards, while traditional coding, debugging, and testing consume a significant portion of their time. Complex legacy code also slows productivity.
  • How the platform helps: Leveraging AI capabilities directly within ABAP Cloud Development, using tools like SAP Joule for Developers, can dramatically increase developer productivity. AI assists with code generation, ensures code quality, and helps your development teams understand complex systems, freeing them to focus on innovation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Find out how AI agents with ABAP simplify migration, speed up coding, and increase productivity, even in older versions of SAP S/4HANA. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • ABAP Cloud: Entering the new era of ABAP development | BTP2107: The ABAP Cloud development model, agentic development experience in VS Code, the SAP Joule for Developers add-on, and agentic custom code migration join forces for the next era of growth in ABAP development tools. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Learn what ABAP Cloud is, get insights into the latest technology advancements, and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

4. Modernize On-Premise Integration to Cloud with PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite Migration

  • The Challenge: Organizations still using SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (SAP PI/PO) are facing the impending need to modernize the integration methods. With a complex integration landscape comprising multiple integration solutions, not only does it add complexity, but it also creates greater technical debt.
  • How the platform helps: Migrating from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite provides a modern, cloud-native platform that reduces Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) across the integration landscape. This transition can cut the time to set up new interfaces and unlock significant annual cost savings. It's a strategic move that not only streamlines operations but also provides the scalable, agile integration backbone essential for future AI-driven cloud transformation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Modernize your strategy: Migrating from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite | BTP1360: Learn how the suite enables smooth, scalable, and intelligent integrations across cloud and hybrid landscapes. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Under Armour: Enabling real-time commerce with SAP Integration Suite | BTP2138: Under Armour is consolidating MuleSoft, Boomi, and SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Modernize from SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration | BTP2100: Learn about migration approaches, coexistence options, and governance—plus the benefits customers see after moving, such as faster change cycles and better reliability and monitoring.
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

Mastering Your Platform Journey: Measure, Optimize, and Guide


To truly maximize your investment in SAP’s Platform, it’s crucial to understand where your organization currently stands and how to strategically advance. Sapphire 2026 will highlight the tools and methodologies designed to guide this journey, available on SAP Discovery Center: 

  • SAP Discovery Center: As your central hub for knowledge and hands-on learning, the Discovery Center continues to evolve. Discover, evaluate, and adopt SAP’s Platform and AI to create tailored business solutions and accelerate your platform adoption.

  • Maturity Assessment: Introduced at last year’s SAP TechEd, this comprehensive technical evaluation transforms uncertainty into actionable insights. In just 10-15 minutes, you can get a precise understanding of your organization's platform maturity across five levels, from "Getting Started" to "Center of Excellence." It provides a clear roadmap, justifies further investments, and uncovers hidden efficiencies and innovation opportunities.

We have since expanded the portfolio with new Line of Business AI assessments for domain-specific insights, and an AI Maturity Calculator is in development with IDC that not only assesses your platform AI readiness but also benchmarks you against your industry peers.

  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Your platform journey: Maturity assessment and Adoption Best Practices | BTP1549: Explore the new maturity assessment that benchmarks your current state and uncovers optimization opportunities, paired with the proven platform adoption framework for SAP BTP that guides implementation.  
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • BTP101: Comprehensive Overview of SAP’s Platform — Foundations, Development, Integration, and Governance | ASUG105: This intensive 6-hour workshop provides a complete foundational overview of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and its core capabilities. Participants will explore the BTP ecosystem, from architecture and development frameworks to enterprise integration and data management solutions.
      🗓️ADD NOW

Your Strategic Next Steps at Sapphire 2026: Attend Must-Attend Sessions:

This Sapphire, don't just attend – Seize this opportunity to proactively empower your organization by understanding the strategic future of AI. In addition to use-case specific recommendations, I’ve identified essential sessions to deepen your strategic understanding.

Secure your spot: add these pivotal sessions to your agenda to fully grasp SAP’s AI and platform vision for enterprise innovation.

  • Scaling AI in cloud ERP with SAP's platform | BTP1343: Learn how the AI platform helps your AI in cloud ERP move from experiments to real, repeatable results. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Using autonomous finance to jump-start an intelligent enterprise | BTP1345: Hear how SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build solutions, and the SAP Business Data Cloud solution offer the framework to orchestrate cross-enterprise processes, helping them steer the corporate growth plan. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • SAP's AI platform: What's new, what's next, and why it matters | BTP1448: Get a tour of the latest innovations, hands‑on tools for developers and business users, and an early preview of upcoming capabilities shaping the future of AI on the platform. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Design the future of enterprise AI agents with Joule Studio | BTP1449: Discover how Joule Studio enables teams to shape the next era of intelligent enterprises. With an intuitive no‑code experience, create secure, context‑aware AI agents that plan, reason, and act across SAP solutions and beyond.
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • From idea to impact: Orchestrating across the AI agent lifecycle | BTP1450: Get a tour of the latest innovations, hands‑on tools for developers and business users, and an early preview of upcoming capabilities shaping the future of AI on the platform. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • The enterprise path to agentic AI | BTP1454: Discover how Joule Studio creates AI solutions from extensions to agentic apps. SAP’s strategy delivers an enterprise-grade intent-to-execution platform that is purpose-built for SAP customers. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Unlock platform value with AI at Cardinal Health and Johnson Controls | BTP1959: Hear how Cardinal Health Inc. and Johnson Controls International plc unlock platform value with AI-enabled application development, integration, automation, and AI services.
    🗓️ADD NOW

See you there!

Sapphire 2026 is your opportunity to discover how SAP’s unified platform can propel your organization forward to AI adoption, helping you become a truly connected enterprise with governance, ready for whatever the future holds. If you’re at Sapphire, please come see me at booth 152 – BTP1958 and let’s chat!

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements
  8. Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
  9. Interactive Value Journey (IVJ)
  10. POD Notifications
  11. New POD 2.0 Framework: Tips and Tricks

POD 2.0 Tips and Tricks

In this blog post, I will share some tips and tricks so you can make the best out of POD 2.0.

Cut, Copy, and Paste

POD 2.0 supports Cut, Copy and Paste of elements, so you can easily duplicate them and move them around.

Use HTML Element to Draw SVGs

You can use 'HTML' element to easily draw SVGs, besides only pure HTML code, and dynamically manipulate them.

Simple Example:

Content: <svg width="100" height="100"><circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40" fill="green" /></svg>

* Switch off 'Sanitize Content'.

Dynamic Example:

You can make it dynamic, by using POD Context values, for example, see example below.

Content: <svg width="100" height="100"><circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40" fill="{=${/execution/operationActivity/selected/0/statusComplete} ? 'green': 'red'}" /></svg>

See below how I used it in a real demo, to create virtual tanks and manipulate their levels, using SVG.

Use iFrame Element to Embed Other Apps

You can easily use the iFrame element to embed other DM apps, like Manage Orders app, including query string filters on top of POD Context values.

1st, add an IFrame element to your POD.

Then, configure 'Source URL'.

You can make 'Source URL' dynamic, using query string on top of POD Context values.

Example: https://assetmagmt.execution.eu20-quality.web.dmc.cloud.sap/cp.portal/site#ManageOrders-Display?sap-ui-app-id-hint=sap.dm.dme.manageorder&&materialFilter={/workList/lastSelected/material}

Busy Indicator

Use the 'Busy Indicator' Action to show a 'processing...' busy indicator on any control or on the whole page/dialog, to provide user feedback and prevent the user from pressing any buttons twice, for example.

Message Box

Use 'Message Box' Action to show a message box with a customized message out of the box, which can include POD Context variables.

Confirm Action

Use 'Confirm' Action to show a confirmation dialog, which can include POD Context variables.

Using 'Confirm', you can add a confirmation action before calling any other subsequent actions, like a production process for example, prompting the user to confirm before calling the production process itself. If the user cancels it, it skips executing all the actions after it.

The confirmation dialog can include POD Context variables.

Focus Element

Use 'Focus Element' Action to set the focus on any element on the page / dialog, which can be very useful when creating custom dialog, barcode scanner integration, handling forms and much more.

This blog will be further updated with more tips and tricks as we go, stay tuned. 🙂

Have you tried POD 2.0 Notifications? Let me know!

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Cost Optimization in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Introduction:

One of the largest cost drivers I noticed within our SAP BTP services was SAP Datasphere. This immediately made me question why the cost was so high and what options we had to optimize it. At first, I found SAP BTP’s cost model difficult to understand, it isn’t straightforward, and getting a full picture requires quite a bit of reading and exploration.

The BTP Administrator Learning Journey helped clarify the different cost models SAP uses, as well as how to analyze spending through the Cost & Usage section in the Global Account. That helped understand the cost incurring for different services in BTP. 

From my experience, many BTP services offer limited opportunities for cost reduction. However, there are services such as Datasphere, HANA Cloud Database, and SAP ABAP Environment where optimization can make a meaningful difference.

In this article, I will focus specifically on how to optimize costs for SAP Datasphere tenants.

As many of you may already know, the cost of an SAP Datasphere tenant is driven primarily by its underlying SAP HANA Cloud Database. This means your Datasphere configuration especially how it allocates and consumes Capacity Units (CUs) directly influences your overall cost.

What is Capacity Unit? 

A Capacity Unit represents a fixed amount of memory and computing resources used for a specific service in SAP BTP you allocate it as quota.

You can estimate number CUs required for your work loads based on Datasphere estimator. 

https://datasphere-estimator-sac-saceu10.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/

Tenant Configuration

Always ensure that your Datasphere tenant is right‑sized based on the specific needs of each environment. This is one of the biggest factors that determines whether your costs go up or stay optimized. Collaborate with the relevant teams to understand the actual resource requirements.

Your sizing will largely depend on how many spaces your BI/Data teams use in Datasphere and the amount of memory and disk capacity allocated to those spaces. These requirements directly influence how you configure your tenant and, ultimately, how much you spend.

In this example, you can see that memory and disk were allocated to certain spaces even though they were not actually being used. To avoid unnecessary cost, make sure you understand how much memory and disk each space truly requires, and then allocate resources accordingly typically adding around 50% headroom to accommodate potential growth or unexpected usage.

Keep in mind that over provisioning resources can create challenges later. While some resources, like CPU, can be reduced easily, others like Memory, Disk may require more disruptive actions. In certain cases, if you significantly over provision, you may even need to recreate the tenant to downsize properly. Allocating only what is needed plus a reasonable buffer helps you avoid these complications and keeps your costs optimized.

When configuring your Datasphere tenant, it’s important to choose the right Performance Class based on your workload whether it is memory intensive, compute intensive, or a balance of both. After selecting the appropriate class, size your disk, memory, and compute resources to match your actual requirements.

Be aware that enabling features such as Elastic Compute Nodes will further increase your overall cost. The primary cost drivers in Datasphere are Memory, CPU, and Disk, so these are the areas where right‑sizing matters most.

You can also enable Multi‑Availability Zone, which adds resilience and high availability. The good news is that this feature is included at no additional cost.

How do you Monitor & right size? 

There are a couple of ways to monitor your Datasphere usage:

1. System Monitor:
This provides real‑time visibility into how much memory and disk resources your Datasphere tenant is currently consuming.

 

2. HANA Cloud Database (Usage History):
If you need historical insights, you can review usage data directly in the underlying HANA Cloud Database. Keep in mind that historical data retention is limited typically you can only view data from approximately the past month.

Here if you see Memory: Max used 76 GB, avg 58 GB

Here if you see CPU: Max used 95% occasionally but Avg is less than 5%

Conclusion:

Using this information, you can collaborate with your BI/Data teams to review both the current and historical usage of your Datasphere tenant. This will help you make informed decisions about right‑sizing the environment, which can significantly reduce your overall costs.

Thank you for taking the time to read my blog. I’d love to hear your thoughts feel free to share any feedback!

Thanks,
Raghu

entry.title: Post-Hiring
entry.content:
Welcome to the last blog in our series on using SAP Fieldglass features to detect candidate fraud. We've covered the candidate selection, interview preparation, hiring process, and offer and start phases. Now let's look at the post-hiring stage. 

Post-hiring is a follow-up verification step that helps detect fraudulent activity that may have passed through the previous phases. It's sometimes overlooked, but it's an important part of a secure hiring process.   

Let's see how SAP Fieldglass features can help you detect if the wrong candidates gained access to internal systems, data, or sensitive information. 

Reasons with Ratings 

Closing a worker is a critical step for detecting candidate fraud because it turns post-hiring from a simple administrative task into a final compliance check.  By integrating this step with data validation, you can detect fraud late in the process, such as identity misrepresentation, unauthorized contract, or scenarios where the hired candidate isn’t the one performing the work. 

When closing a worker, you can define a specific rating to evaluate their performance. You can close a worker when their work order ends, if they leave an assignment early, or if they don't show up. 

Close ratings are Positive, Neutral, and Negative. Evaluating a worker with a negative rating can help in a future selection process by indicating that this specific worker may not be a good option for rehiring. 

The following are some aspects you should consider when rating a worker who displayed suspicious behavior: 

  • Poor performance inconsistent with what was presented during the interview 
  • Avoiding meetings or camera 
  • Sudden disappearances 
  • Requesting excessive system access 
  • Inability to perform basic tasks claimed as expertise 

Do Not Rehire Flag 

During the post-hiring phase, you can flag candidates who didn't display appropriate behavior. The Do Not Rehire flag helps you inform hiring managers and everyone involved in the selection process that this candidate shouldn’t be rehired. 

You can define and set this flag based on past performance and policy compliance, reducing the risk of hiring fraudulent candidates and helping to maintain workplace safety.   

The following are important points about the Do Not Rehire flag: 

  • Activation: The flag is commonly linked to a Negative rating when a worker is closed 
  • Display: It appears in the top portion of the employee file for visibility 
  • Application: It works as an alert throughout the recruitment process when a job seeker is presented 
  • Setup: If the flag isn’t appearing on a candidate profile, this might be related to how the closing reason is configured. It typically requires a Negative rating to be attached to it 

This feature helps improve compliance during the offboarding process and can assist you during your post-hiring verification. 

We've reached the last blog in our series. Thank you for joining us!   

In this five-part series, we shared tips and insights to help you identify and address fraudulent candidates during your selection process. We hope these features and techniques help you build a more secure and reliable hiring workflow.   

Remember, every genuine candidate you successfully identify is a win for your organization's future. So, stay vigilant and best of luck in building a trustworthy and talented team!

 

 

 

For the purposes of this guide, "candidate fraud" means deliberate misrepresentation of identity, credentials, work history, or qualifications during recruitment. This guide is preventive and educational; it does not make legal determinations.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026 - AI database for AI agents and apps: Overview of SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!

My name is Daniel Dukes, and I’m on SAP’s Technical Product Marketing team. My primary focus is SAP HANA Cloud. Day to day, I spend a lot of time evangelizing the practicality of our database platform at events and through content published in our Data Professionals community. I’ve been in the SAP ecosystem for close to 25 years with the last 15 years at SAP. Much of my tenure has involved working with in-memory data technology in one form or another – from the SAP BW Accelerator to SAP HANA Cloud. 

Database @ SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando

I am really looking forward to this year in Orlando. Here is a “sneak peek” of my session at 1:00 PM EDT on Tuesday, May 12 - AI database for AI agents and apps: Overview of SAP HANA Cloud | BDC2188.

The current plan is to walk through the AI-related features in SAP HANA Cloud from two angles. The first topic covers how HANA Cloud can enable AI scenarios - e.g., as the data layer for agents and generative AI services. I also plan to review how HANA Cloud uses AI to improve developer and user productivity. Along with an overview of the solution, I also want to discuss the kinds of use cases this database can empower.

Additionally, here are some other highly recommended SAP HANA Cloud sessions: 

  • Share calculation views and data products across SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC | BDC2187 @ 2:00 PM EDT on Tuesday, May 12
  • Build smarter AI agents with an agent memory service in SAP HANA Cloud | BDC 7224 @ 3:00 PM EDT on Tuesday, May 12
  • Semos Cloud delivers culture intelligence with AI agents on SAP HANA Cloud | BDC2632 @ 2:30 PM EDT on Wednesday, May 13
  • SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud | BDC2947 @ Demo Station

Discover, Connect, Learn, and Grow

If you are ready to explore SAP HANA Cloud beyond these experiences, the quickest hands-on starting point is the SAP HANA Cloud basic trial. It’s a straightforward way to try features yourself without needing a full landscape. After that, I’d suggest working through the hands-on tutorials within the Discovery Center. A third great recommendation is to join other Data Professionals in the SAP Community. Feel free to participate in a place built for those who work with data.

Share Your Thoughts and Experiences

Until we meet in person, let’s correspond through the SAP community. Consider posting a reply to the following question. I’d appreciate any concrete examples (even small ones) shared in the comments section. 

Prompt: Where do you see AI adding real value in your data platform work today?

~

Looking forward to meeting you at my session or on the show floor 😊

entry.title: Re: Task list operations selection within PM orders
entry.content:
In SAP PM/EAM, there are multiple ways to assign task lists to work order operations, and the best approach often depends on whether you are designing new task lists or working within an existing setup.

If you are in a design, redesign, or migration phase, you can structure task lists more effectively by:

  • Using equipment or functional location–specific task lists to drive contextual selection
  • Creating dummy equipment/functional locations to logically group and narrow down large task list volumes
  • Leveraging work center alignment for easier filtering during selection
  • Using general task lists with selection profiles to guide planners
  • Maintaining external group names for contractor or third-party task lists

However, in many real-world scenarios, task lists are already in production and cannot be easily restructured. In such cases, options are more limited, but one practical and low-impact improvement is to standardize the Group Counter Description field which is avialble in the header section of tasklist and also during selection option when adding to work Order>IW31/IW32>Extras>Tasklist Selection>General Tasklist> then you get the selection screen>Short Text>"Enter New Naming String with *MOTOR*"

Instead of keeping it generic, you can introduce a naming convention in the tasklist counter description to improve usability and searchability.

Example naming format:
[Asset Type] – [Maintenance Type] – [Frequency/Scope] – [Team/Area]

Examples:

  • PUMP – PM – MONTHLY – NORTH
  • TRANSFORMER – INSPECTION – ANNUAL – ELEC
  • VALVE – CORRECTIVE – FIELD – GAS OPS
  • MOTOR – LUBRICATION – QUARTERLY – MECH

This approach helps planners quickly identify the right task list during selection without requiring structural changes to existing task lists.

Example (MRO Task List):
Task List Group: MRO_001
Group Counter Description: PUMP – PM – STANDARD – MECH

Overall, if restructuring is not feasible, improving naming standards (especially Group Counter Description) is an effective way to enhance task list selection and usability in SAP.

Hope this helps !

entry.title: Re: Report FINS_SWITCH_XLGCLR in Public Cloud / Field OI...
entry.content:
Dear @JosipaP,

In Public Cloud there is no customer-accessible self-service report equivalent to FINS_SWITCH_XLGCLR for changing Clearing Specific to Ledger Groups / Open Item Management by Ledger Group. For standard Open Item Management changes the supported Public Cloud process is to use Schedule Accounting Data Changes F4622 with job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts. SAP confirms that in Public Cloud this change must be executed through this job template rather than directly in the G/L account master data

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2969261

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/c8d4d165bdd24fbf9adf0a061966c19a.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4622')/S36

For Open Item Management by Ledger Group the restriction is stricter. SAP confirms that if ledger-group-specific journal entries have already been posted to the G/L account, normal Open Item Management cannot be activated anymore for that account. This is standard system design. The supported resolution is to create a new G/L account with normal OIM activated and use it for future transactions

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3659519

Summary:

FINS_SWITCH_XLGCLR should not be positioned as a Public Cloud option. In Public Cloud, F4622 is the supported tool for standard OIM changes. For blocked changes related to Clearing Specific to Ledger Groups / OIM by Ledger Group the SAP-supported approach is to create a new G/L account with the required setting or raise an SAP incident if SAP needs to assess a tenant-specific correction

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Prefer:odata.continue-on-error - Not working in ODATA V4 BATCH API call
entry.content:
Hi,

While Postman correctly returns both success and error responses for all payloads when the Prefer: odata.continue-on-error header is used, this same header fails to function as expected within the Fiori app's AJAX call. 

Case 1 : Suppose if all 5 payloads are successful, API gives a response for all 5 payloads .

Case 2: Total 3 payloads,  1st payload response is successful, 2  payload response fails with 400 error and skips 3rd payload response.




CAse 2 analysis - The API continues to return the first successful response and the second failed response, but then skips the third payload entirely.

FIORI APP SOURCE CODE

var URL = "/sap/opu/odata4/sap/-------------------/";

var msgarr = [];

$.ajax({

    url: URL,

    type: "POST",

    contentType: "multipart/mixed;boundary=batch",

    headers: {

        "X-CSRF-Token": UCSRFToken,

        "Prefer": "odata.continue-on-error"

    },

    data: sPayload,

    success: function (data, textStatus, jqXHR) {

        var a = data;

        var sResponse = jqXHR.responseText;

    }

});

Issue Summary: Why is the Prefer header not working in SAP Web IDE? Despite adding the header to the code above, the application still terminates processing after a payload failure, skipping subsequent responses.

Thanks,

Madhava

entry.title: Trying to connect to an SSL-encrypted SAP ASE 16.0 database using PowerBuilder
entry.content:
Trying to connect to an SAP ASE 16.0 database using PowerBuilder. The database is using SSL encryption. I can connect to other databases on the same server that are not SSL-encrypted. I can connect to the SSL-encrypted database using Aqua Data Studio, which has you use a tool to add the trusted certificate into its cacert. PowerBuilder just has you add ",ssl" to the entries referencing the database in sql.ini, and you are to add the trusted.txt to the folder where the sql.ini is located. I have done that, but I am getting the following error when trying to connect:

ct_connect(): network packet layer: internal net library error: Failed to initialize network filter.

Can you either tell me what needs to change, or point me to the download for ASE 16 client so that I can test connecting with isql? (This is a remote Windows machine trying to connect to a Linux database server .)

entry.title: Re: How to process Mass retirement of assets in SAP S4 Ha...
entry.content:
Dear @Cheng_Huang58,

Sorry for replying today, I did not receive a notification for your comment. I agree that mass asset retirement can be a standard operational requirement. In Public Cloud the requirement is not ignored,but it is delivered through a different model than the classic AR01 / AR31 worklist transactions. In the general Asset Accounting scope, SAP provides Post Asset Retirement F7107 for manual retirement postings and the released Fixed Asset Post Asset Retirement API / API_FIXEDASSETRETIREMENT_G4BA for mass or automated processing. This indicates that Public Edition supports the business requirement, but the intended mass-processing pattern is API-based rather than a classic embedded GUI worklist

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/560ade3edf964466b10cc918c50cfa8e.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F7107')/S36

The embedded Mass Processing of Assets app is not positioned by SAP as a general replacement for AR01 / AR31. SAP confirms that this app belongs to the specific Oil & Gas / JVA scope and is available only when the relevant scope/licensing is active

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3219541

Therefore the additional rationale is a combination of scope, product packaging and Public Cloud delivery model. AR01 / AR31 remain classic worklist transactions from the on-premise/private-edition model while Public Edition provides a standard posting app plus a released API for controlled mass automation

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: how to add the fields in the maintain email template app
entry.content:
we have PO approval and rejection email template. we need to add the plant field in the template but it is not available in the data fields. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to add the fields in the maintain email template app
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the fields (or variables) available in PO workflow email templates are strictly limited to those exposed in the predefined template data source.

As confirmed in SAP KBA :  Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence 

  • If a field (e.g., Plant) or variable (e.g., Workflow Task URL) is not available in the data source, it cannot be added to the email template
  • This is standard system behavior, not a configuration issue
  • Any missing field is considered a new requirement / feature gap

Current Limitation

  • PO email templates do not expose all business fields (e.g., Plant, workflow URL)
  • Only selected objects (like Supplier Invoice or PR) have richer variables
  • No extensibility is available to add custom or standard fields into the template data source

This is a known gap and is being tracked via SAP Influence requests (e.g., 304655  Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence and  Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence), and any enhancement depends on SAP roadmap decisions. 

Best Regards, 
SAP Support

entry.title: AI Q&A Agent for FRE Transport & Bundle Adoption: From Wikis to Conversational Knowledge
entry.content:
Overview

How we built a conversational Q&A Agent for the FRE (First Run Experience) team at SAP, purpose-trained on Transport Management and Bundle Adoption knowledge, using Claude via SAP Hyperspace AI proxy and the Anthropic SDK.

The goal is to give application teams a fast, interactive way to understand FRE Transport and Bundle concepts before they start adoption work, without having to go through wikis, design docs, recordings, and Git repos.

Why We Built This

Onboarding a new application team to FRE adoption involves understanding a lot of context spread across multiple sources including transport lifecycle, bundle types, adoption repo setup, and Temporal workflows. A Q&A agent lets teams ask natural questions and get structured answers instantly, reducing back and forth with the FRE team.   

Architecture

Key Design Decisions

1. Knowledge base as structured context: The full domain knowledge is embedded directly in the system prompt as a structured markdown document. This works well for a bounded domain with no RAG or vector store needed.    

2. Smart caching with fuzzy matching: Exact match via MD5 hash gives instant replay for repeated questions. Fuzzy match via Jaccard similarity catches near duplicate phrasing. The cache persists across restarts via .qa-cache.json.

3. Continuous learning: Each interaction is stored in .qa-learned.json with up to 100 entries and a 14 day TTL, then injected back into the system prompt so the agent improves within and across sessions. 

4. Hyperspace AI proxy: Uses SAP's internal Hyperspace AI desktop app as a local proxy on http://localhost:6655/anthropic so no direct API key management is needed for SAP developers.                 

5. Adaptive thinking: Uses thinking with type adaptive so Claude reasons through complex questions before answering, which is especially useful for multi step workflow questions.

What the Agent Knows

Transport Management

  • All 16 DB level transport request statuses and their UI mapping 
  • Full CTMS flow from upload to export to import to GACD deploy
  • Error scenarios and validation rules on transport request creation
  • Config level statuses

Bundle, Transport and Adoption

  • Day0 (Configuration Bundle) vs Day 1 (Transport Bundle)
  • 3 tier vs 2 tier landscape transport rules
  • All 3 adoptionType options (freProvidedAPI, custom, odata) with examples
  • CDS annotations and bundle-config-metadata.json field reference                                                                                                   

Adoption Repo Setup

  • processes from fre-installation script to production ready repository
  • Temporal (cobalttemporal) configuration in mta.yaml
  •  fre-client-library setup and local dev port layout

 SyncMetadata Job

  • 10 minute BTP Job Scheduler trigger
  • Full sync flow through BundleControllerService with MD5 change detection

Try It Yourself 

Prerequisites: Node.js 18+ and SAP Hyperspace AI desktop app running

git clone https://github.tools.sap/First-Run-Experience/fre-srv/blob/rupa_qa_agent_transport/

cd fre-srv/qa-agent

npm install

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=<from Hyperspace AI settings>

npm start

Example questions to try

What are all the transport request statuses?

How does the SyncMetadata job work? 

What is the difference between freProvidedAPI and custom adoption type?

How do I configure Temporal in mta.yaml

What errors can occur when creating a transport request?

How does fre-srv trigger adoption repo workflows?

Current Scope: Trained on transport design docs, adoption template wikis, fre-srv, suite-adoption, adoption-template and client-library repos, and UI demo recordings. Answers stay grounded in the knowledge base and out of scope questions are acknowledged clearly. Local CLI only for now.
